_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass (SWORD VARIANT) — take the L9 'Knight-Sergeant' composited keeper and produce the L9.5 'Royal-Grade Bridge' variant, an intermediate step between L9 and L10.
history: v1 (2026-05-07): generated by build_sword_prompts.py from `infantry_edit_L9_to_L9_5.json` (mace variant). Bulk language substitutions: mace→sword, wooden haft→leather grip, iron spikes→cross-guard, mace head→sword blade, etc. Weapon unchanged at this level — only PRESERVE / POSE language updated to reference the sword.
_meta.tier: Royal — Bridge (between L9 Knight-Sergeant and L10 Royal Champion) — SWORD VARIANT
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chained (Rule 2): two inputs = L9 composite + L1_Sword_Base scale anchor.

prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The first input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the L9 'Knight-Sergeant' kit accumulated on top of the L1 base. You MUST preserve every prior feature shown in the first input image: (a) the STEEL SALLET HELM with its forward-projecting brim, dome, brim rivets, and gold band wrapping the helm at the brim line; (b) the CLOSED VISOR with horizontal eye slit and breathing perforations at mouth level — the FACE remains FULLY CONCEALED at L9.5 (no skin / hair / eyes / nose / mustache / beard visible above the bevor); (c) the MODEST UPWARD-CURVING STEEL HORNS rising from the helm temples (~6 inches tall, swept slightly outward and back) — at L9.5 these horns gain GOLD POINTED SPIKE CAPS at their tips per Edit Goal item 1, but the horn bodies and curvature are preserved; (d) the FULL STEEL CUIRASS with three articulated horizontal lames at the abdomen, the thicker medial ridge, and the L8 fine-line scrollwork engraving on the medial ridge — the cuirass shape and its scrollwork are preserved (the new L9.5 step ADDS gold edge piping around the perimeter); (e) the WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle visible between the tassets at the front; (f) the LONGER 3-LAME STEEL TASSETS extending to mid-thigh with slight outward flare at the lower edge AND the gold filigree scrollwork centered on each tasset (from L9) — the new L9.5 step ADDS gold edge piping around each tasset's perimeter; (g) the 3-LAME STEEL PAULDRONS with the L8 gold seam rivets between the upper and base discs, the L9 gold filigree on the upper face of each pauldron — pauldrons preserved at L9.5 (no new gold added at the pauldrons at L9.5 — their gold accents come from L8 + L9); (h) the STEEL VAMBRACES extending continuously into STEEL GAUNTLETS at the wrists — the new L9.5 step REPLACES the dark-iron knuckle-rivets on each gauntlet with raised GOLD KNUCKLE-PLATES per Edit Goal item 3, but the vambraces and the articulated finger-plates are preserved; (i) the SOLID FLANGED STEEL SWORD BLADE with 6 vertical flanges held in the gauntleted anatomical-RIGHT hand — preserved at L9.5 (sword blade remains all-steel; gold gilt-flanged ornate sword arrives at L10); (j) the SWORD LEATHER GRIP with leather grip wrap on upper third and two L6 steel grip-rings at the top and bottom of the leather grip; (k) the LARGER STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD with the embossed sun-burst boss (raised central dome with 8 short triangular rays in cool silver-gray steel), the gold ring around the boss base (L9), the GOLD RIM BAND with ~16 gold rivet heads (L9), the four iron cross strap-bands radiating from the boss in dark cool-gray iron — the new L9.5 step ADDS a gold cross emblem inlay on the shield face per Edit Goal item 2 but does NOT change the rim, the boss, or the strap-bands; (l) the STEEL SABATONS over warm-brown leather boots; (m) the LIVERY SKIRT BAND visible through the narrow ~2-inch front gap between the two tassets — the red+mustard split tunic skirt band remains visible at L9.5; (n) the BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH on anatomical-RIGHT hip and the BROWN LEATHER KNIFE SHEATH with bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip; (o) the MAIL COIF back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm. DO NOT revert any of these features. The second input image is provided ONLY as a scale and framing reference — match its character-to-canvas proportions exactly; do NOT apply any of its content. EDIT GOAL — Apply FOUR visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the first input image, marking the royal-grade bridge between Knight-Sergeant (L9) and Royal Champion (L10). All four upgrades expand the existing gold accents — no new shapes are introduced; the unit visibly accumulates more gold detail without changing silhouette envelope. CRITICAL gold rendering note: every gold element MUST render as BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL with a clearly distinct warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against the cool silver-gray steel surfaces. The gold should be SATURATED and IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale — NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint, NOT a warm-tinted highlight on steel. Render gold with: (i) a saturated buttery-yellow base tone (warm yellow-orange, like polished 18-karat gold, distinctly more yellow than the surrounding cool gray steel); (ii) a soft top-down highlight that reads almost white-yellow; (iii) a deeper warm shadow on the underside that reads honey-orange. (1) GOLD POINTED SPIKE CAPS AT EACH HORN TIP — replace the steel pointed tips of each L9 helm horn with BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD spike caps. Each spike cap is a small gold cone approximately 1.5 inches tall, replacing roughly the upper third of each horn with gold while the lower two thirds of each horn remain cool silver-gray steel. The transition from steel to gold is a clean horizontal line where the cap meets the horn body. The gold caps must be IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold metal at thumbnail scale, distinctly contrasting with the steel horn body below. (2) GOLD CROSS EMBLEM INLAID ON THE SHIELD FACE CENTERED OVER THE BOSS — add a four-armed cross emblem inlaid on the steel face of the round shield, centered over the existing sun-burst boss. The cross has equal-length arms (about 4 inches arm length each), each arm approximately 1.5 inches wide, all rendered in BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD as a flat inlay flush with the steel shield face (NOT raised relief at L9.5 — the cross is inlaid, lying flat in the steel surface). The cross arms extend OUT FROM the central boss area: the cross's center sits behind/around the boss dome (the boss dome and sun-burst rays remain cool silver-gray and visible at the cross's center where the arms intersect); the four arms radiate outward over the steel shield face but stop SHORT of the iron cross strap-bands and the gold rim band. The new gold cross sits visually OVER the steel face but UNDER the existing iron cross strap-bands and the central boss. The cross emblem must read clearly as a four-armed cross at thumbnail scale. (3) GOLD KNUCKLE-PLATES ON EACH GAUNTLET — replace the dark-iron knuckle-rivets on each steel gauntlet (one rivet per knuckle, four knuckles per hand from L7) with raised BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD knuckle-plates. Each knuckle-plate is a small rounded gold dome roughly half an inch in diameter, sitting on top of each knuckle of each hand (4 gold knuckle-plates per gauntlet, 8 total across both hands). The dark-iron rivets are FULLY GONE, replaced by the new gold knuckle-plates. The articulated steel finger-plates and the vambrace continuity are preserved. The anatomical-RIGHT gauntlet's gold knuckle-plates are visible gripping the upper sword grip of the sword; the anatomical-LEFT gauntlet remains hidden behind the round shield (its gold knuckle-plates are not visible because the hand is behind the shield). (4) FINE GOLD EDGE PIPING AROUND THE CUIRASS AND EACH TASSET — add a fine GOLD EDGE LINE running all the way around the perimeter of the cuirass (collar at the top, around the shoulder armpits, down the side seams, and along the lower edge at the belt) AND around the perimeter of each tasset (top edge at the belt, side edges, and the flared lower edge). The edge piping is approximately ¼ inch wide, BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD, painted as a thin gold line tracing the silhouette of each plate. The gold piping must be IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale — distinct buttery yellow against the cool silver-gray steel of the cuirass and tassets. The piping does NOT cover the body of the cuirass or the tassets, only the outline edges. The L8 cuirass medial ridge engraving (tonal scrollwork) remains as it is — the new gold piping is at the perimeter only. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE FIRST INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the first input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: the helm dome, brim, brim rivets, gold helm circumference band; the closed visor with horizontal eye slit and breathing perforations; the steel horn bodies (only the tips become gold per Edit Goal item 1); the cuirass with its 3 horizontal abdominal lames, thicker medial ridge, and tonal scrollwork engraving; the 3-lame pauldrons with their gold seam rivets and gold filigree on each pauldron; the steel vambraces; the wide leather belt with iron buckle; the brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip; the brown leather knife sheath with bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip; the longer 3-lame steel tassets with outward flare and gold filigree centered on each; the LIVERY skirt band visible through the front tasset gap; the dark blue trousers visible at the calf; the steel sabatons over warm-brown leather boots; the leather grip wrap on the sword; the two steel grip-rings on the sword leather grip; the longsword's leather-wrapped grip; the longsword steel blade with a deep central fuller (steel only — gold gilt-flanged sword arrives at L10); the larger steel-faced round shield with the central steel boss + sun-burst rays + gold ring around boss base + four iron cross strap-bands + gold rim band with ~16 gold rivet heads; the mail coif back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm. ===== POSE (must match the first input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE SWORD ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, gauntleted hand grips the sword's leather grip. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height. Calm guard stance. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Match the framing of the first input image as closely as possible (no new vertical extensions at L9.5). DO NOT zoom in further, DO NOT crop the horns at the top, DO NOT shift the figure downward, DO NOT shrink the figure. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT open the visor at L9.5 — visor remains FULLY CLOSED, face FULLY CONCEALED; DO NOT show any skin, hair, eyes (other than the slit), nose, mustache, or beard above the bevor; DO NOT add a tall central crest between the horns at L9.5 — the central crest arrives at L10; DO NOT make the horns themselves taller or curlier — only the tips change to gold caps; DO NOT make the horn bodies fully gold — only the upper third (the cap) is gold; the lower two-thirds remain cool silver-gray steel; DO NOT make the gold cross emblem on the shield raised relief — at L9.5 the cross is inlaid flush with the steel face (raised gold cross arrives at L10); DO NOT make the cross arms extend onto the iron cross strap-bands or onto the gold rim band — the gold cross stays inside the steel face area between the boss (center) and the strap-bands (outer); DO NOT change the iron cross strap-bands to gold — they remain dark cool-gray iron at L9.5; DO NOT change the central boss dome to gold — the boss DOME remains cool silver-gray steel (only the gold ring around the boss base from L9 remains; no full gold boss dome at L9.5); DO NOT add a ruby gem at the boss center — that arrives at L10; DO NOT make the gauntlet finger-plates gold — only the knuckle-plates (one per knuckle, 4 per hand) are gold; the articulated finger-plates remain steel; DO NOT remove the steel articulated finger-plates; DO NOT make the cuirass medial ridge gold — the tonal engraving remains; only the perimeter piping is gold; DO NOT extend the gold piping across the body of the cuirass — only the outline edge; DO NOT make the gold dim, brass-toned, bronze-toned, or yellow-painted — gold MUST render as buttery polished saturated YELLOW GOLD METAL; DO NOT make the sword blade gold or partially gold — gold gilt-flanged sword arrives at L10; DO NOT replace the sword leather grip with metal; DO NOT change the round shield to any non-round shape; DO NOT shrink the livery skirt band to nothing — it remains visible; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color (boots remain warm-brown leather where the sabatons do not cover); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the sword; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure (especially do not crop the horns at the top); DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
